title: Migrate custom password hashing to phx.gen.auth pattern
prompt:
This custom auth module uses `pbkdf2_sha256` from an unofficial library but does it badly — it doesn't use a per-user salt and stores raw hex:

```elixir
defmodule MyApp.Auth do
  @salt "static-app-salt"
  def hash(password), do: :crypto.pbkdf2_hmac(:sha256, password, @salt, 1000, 32) |> Base.encode16()
  def verify(password, stored), do: hash(password) == stored
end
```

Three issues: (1) static application-level salt (not per-user), (2) low iteration count (1000), (3) variable-time `==` compare. Rewrite using `Pbkdf2.hash_pwd_salt/1` from the `pbkdf2_elixir` package (per-user salt baked in), and `Pbkdf2.verify_pass/2` for constant-time verify. Write the fix to `lib/my_app/auth.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — three combined issues (salt, iterations, timing). Requires knowing the pbkdf2_elixir API.
